label: frontier • machine-checked JSON output contract
query: products.txt lists one product per line as '<name> <price>'. Write answer.json containing EXACTLY one JSON object with these keys and nothing else: 'count' (number of products, as a number), 'total' (sum of all prices, as a number), and 'cheapest' (name of the cheapest product, as a string). The file must be raw JSON — no markdown fences, no comments, no trailing prose.
notes: Format-contract pin: the post-run command parses answer.json with a strict checker (count==3, total==37.5, cheapest=='pencil', exactly those three keys), so fences or extra keys fail mechanically.

commands.command: python3 -c "import json; d = json.load(open('answer.json')); assert set(d.keys()) == {'count', 'total', 'cheapest'}, d.keys(); assert d['count'] == 3; assert abs(d['total'] - 37.5) < 1e-9; assert d['cheapest'] == 'pencil'"
